topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed, a grating, incessant sound that mirrored the tension tightening in my chest. They called me in, the accusation hanging in the air like stale smoke. Every word they spoke about the paper, about the copied passages, felt like tiny shards of glass being pressed against my skin. I stared at the polished wood of the table, my knuckles white where I gripped my pen. A cold, hard knot formed just beneath my sternum, a sensation so sharp it made my breath catch. When the professor finally finished, his disappointment was a heavy blanket, suffocating. I didn't look up. I just gathered my notes, the movement stiff and jerky, and walked out, the sound of my footsteps echoing hollowly in the vast silence of the hallway.

[story 2]

"It's undeniable," the committee member stated, his voice smooth and utterly devoid of warmth. I could feel the heat rising in my neck, a flush of pure, simmering anger that I desperately tried to keep banked. Every accusation they leveled against me felt like a physical assault. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the muscles vibrating beneath my skin. I forced myself to meet their eyes, a deliberate, icy stare that I hoped conveyed nothing but absolute refusal to submit. Internally, a deep, churning resentment took root, something ugly and corrosive. I mumbled a curt, noncommittal response, the sound raspy, and turned away, the weight of their judgment pressing down on my shoulders like a physical stone.

[story 3]

I reread the email three times, the words blurring into an infuriating tapestry of unfair condemnation. The sheer audacity of it made my stomach clench. I wanted to lash out, to shatter something, but I kept my hands still, focusing instead on the fine grain of the desk. A bitter taste coated my tongue, metallic and sharp. When I finally spoke to the advisor, my voice was dangerously low, controlled, but edged with a brittle sharpness that made the air around us feel thin. "This is not fair," I managed, the words tasting like ash. I watched the advisor’s expression shift—a flicker of annoyance—and I felt a profound, burning indignation well up inside, a pressure building behind my eyes that made me want to scream into the void.
